_meta.what: REAL FCP PAETint transfer, tint Color=[1,0,0] Intensity=1, on grayscale inputs (isolated via uniform-colour inputs through transfer_batch.py).
_meta.why: Clean isolated data to decode the HgcTint hard-light two-leg transfer + its colour-space/gamma step (the documented ceiling in channel-mixer.ts). Better than prior photo-A probes (pipeline-coupled).
observations: R channel (tint=1): out=3.88*luma for luma<0.25, saturates ~255 by luma>=0.376. Fast highlight lift.
G/B channels (tint=0): ~0 until luma~0.4, then out≈2*luma-1 for luma>0.5 (sRGB shadow leg), with a smoothstep softening near the 0.5 threshold (matches prior RE note smoothstep(~0.27,~1.22,luma)).
Neither pure-sRGB nor pure-linear two-leg fit is exact; G matches sRGB 2*luma-1 well, R slope 3.88 needs leg-by-leg reconstruction.
_meta.next: Reconstruct each leg (shadow/highlight) with the correct sel/smoothstep in sRGB; the G(tint=0) shadow leg = 2*luma-1 is confirmed. Then fix tintFilter + nested Color read TOGETHER and verify Leaves stays >=22.44 on the GUI gate.

decode_progress.disasm_formula_tested: luma=v/255; A=(1-luma)*(tint-1); shadow=2A+1; highl=2*luma*tint-shadow; sel=(luma<0.5); tinted=sel*highl+shadow (sRGB).
decode_progress.result: G channel (tint=0) matches for luma>0.5 (shadow leg=2*luma-1) but the <0.5 transition is SHARPER than FCP (needs the smoothstep the prior RE note found). R channel (tint=1) is ~2x too LOW in shadows (32->pred 64 vs FCP 124.3; factor ~1.94).
hypotheses_for_R_2x: The tinted RGB may be re-premultiplied AND the grayscale luma path double-counts (r0.xyz*=r0.w with a non-1 alpha).
sel/leg selection for tint=1,luma<0.5 may pick a DIFFERENT leg than tint=0 (the highlight leg 2*luma*tint dominates, not shadow).
luma weights: for a pure grayscale input luma=v/255 regardless of 601/709, so it's NOT a luma-weight issue.
decode_progress.status: OPEN — G shadow leg pinned (sRGB 2*luma-1 + smoothstep); R leg + threshold smoothstep still to reconstruct. Full HgcTint decode remains the prerequisite to unblock the Tint fix.

_meta.verbatim_shader: HgcTint_hgc_visible (extracted): r0=color0/alpha; luma=dot(rgb,hgP[2]); A=(1-luma)*(tint-1); shadow=2A+1; highl=2*luma*tint-shadow; sel=(luma<0.5); tinted=sel*highl+shadow; out=mix(rgb,tinted,hgP[1]); out*=alpha.
_meta.ceiling_pinned: The VERBATIM shader in sRGB gives R(tint=1,luma<0.5)=2*luma, but REAL FCP gives ~3.88*luma (32->124 not 64; 64->248 not 128) — a constant ~1.94x MORE than the shader predicts, and it saturates R by luma~0.25. Neither sRGB nor linear space resolves it (linear worse). The 3.88 slope is NOT explained by the verbatim per-pixel math, the Intensity mix (=1, no-op), or the luma weights (grayscale => weight-invariant). Candidates for the missing 2x: (a) the re-premultiply r0*=alpha with a conformed alpha != 1; (b) a colour-space/gamma stage applied to hg_Params[0] (the tint colour) or to the shader in/out that the extracted fragment doesn't show; (c) the shader runs on values in a scaled range. This is the SAME unresolved colour-space step channel-mixer.ts documents as the Tint ceiling. G(tint=0) shadow leg 2*luma-1 IS pinned in sRGB. Full decode still blocked on the R-channel 2x / colour-space stage.
